name: Bert Williams
abstract: Bert Williams (November 12, 1874 – March 4, 1922) was a Bahamian-born American entertainer, one of the pre-eminent entertainers of the vaudeville era and one of the most popular comedians for all audiences of his time. While some sources have credited him as being the first Black man to have a leading role in a film with Darktown Jubilee…

concerts.label: The Plaza Republican Club Annual Reception and Vaudeville

about_quotes.speaker: Jay Cee Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: J. C. mentioned to me that between 1915 and 1918 he was running an elevator in New York City in the building where Bert Williams either lived or worked.

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Wooding
about_quotes.evidence: Now, well, where I got my inspiration was, when we went to this theater, we saw Williams and Walker. Williams Bert Williams was a great comedian...

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: I had acquired a way of talking similar to the late-great Bert Williams... And there was Bert Williams. He was great, you see.

about_quotes.speaker: John W. Bubbles
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Did you know Bert Williams? [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. I met him after I went to New York.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: But she knew Bert Williams. She never worked with him but she knew him.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: They had a lot of Bert Williams records and Paul Whiteman records.
